question: Which one of the following statements regarding workload management is true?

options.text: Effective workload management ensures essential operations are accomplished by planning, prioritizing, and sequencing tasks.
As workload increases, attention should be devoted to cover several tasks at one time.
When a work overload situation exists, a pilot needs to think quickly and make informed decisions.
As experience is gained, a pilot learns to recognize future workload requirements and should delegate the tasks to a less experienced pilot to learn.
